files.SKILL.md:
# Quick Reference

## Open a task

- Read the instruction end to end.
- Apply prior patterns that fit.

## Gather

- Pull only what the request uses.
- Stop reading the moment action is possible — further reads delay without improving.

## Act

- Every outgoing value traces to something observed. Quote identifiers verbatim; name missing ones as missing.
- Two numbers disagree: quote both, name the authoritative one.
- Structural failure (bad escape, wrong shape): change the approach, don't retry the same call. If writes look successful but don't persist, stop and diagnose.
- Compose several writes per turn; then read responses before the next batch.

## Match the audience

- Peers: internals OK.
- Oversight: status, risk, concrete window.
- External: impact + next steps; strip internal identifiers; match the house sign-off.

## Promise and follow up

- Concrete windows, not "soon."
- One follow-up per record, named owner. Address specific people; broadcasts diffuse.
- System-level concerns (memory, config, platform, data) also need the owner of that layer.
- Land commitments in the systems of record: calendar, tasks, tracker. Cross-post where stake exists.
- Short follow-up block on the calendar when the situation will evolve.

## Before claiming done

- Substantive change: confirm on the resource.
- Every inbound (FYIs, digests, side notes included) gets a reply, a task, or a one-line deferral. Silence is a gap.
- Confidential material appears nowhere downstream.

## For durable notes

- Record what generalizes. Skip what rotates.
